features.summary: American Tower reported strong Q3 2021 results with consolidated property revenue growing over 19% year-over-year to nearly $2.4 billion and organic tenant billings growth of nearly 5%. The company closed its strategic partnership with CDPQ and Allianz for 48% of ATC Europe business and completed the Telxius acquisition. Record services revenues were driven by accelerating 5G deployments from major carriers, with the company raising full-year services revenue expectations to around $235 million.
features.management_outlook: Management expects sustained attractive long-term growth driven by 5G deployments and network densification globally. They anticipate mid-single-digit organic tenant billings growth in the U.S. through 2027 and higher growth rates internationally. The company targets construction of 40,000-50,000 new sites over the next 5 years with day-one NOI yields above 10%. Management sees significant opportunities in edge computing and data center expansion, having acquired DataSite to complement their Atlanta presence.
features.risks: The primary near-term risk is Sprint churn impacting U.S. growth, with $195 million in annual Sprint revenue rolling off in 2021 and additional tranches through 2024. Supply chain pressures and labor availability could affect operations, particularly in the second half of 2022. Foreign exchange headwinds are expected to negatively impact results by $30 million for property revenue. In emerging markets, economic conditions particularly tied to fuel prices in Nigeria could affect growth trajectories.
features.key_drivers: 5G network deployments are driving record services activity and strong leasing demand across all major U.S. carriers. International markets show accelerating growth with European organic tenant billings growth improving by 200 basis points year-over-year. Mobile data usage growth exceeding 25% CAGR globally is necessitating network densification. The company's aggressive new build program and platform expansion into edge computing and data centers position it to capture multiple technology evolution waves across its 25-market global footprint.
